situations.title: Greetings & Presence
situations.summary: Automate the greeting loop. Essential for every social interaction in Madeira.

cultural_notes.title: Greetings & Presence
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, greetings are more than just words; they are a social ritual. Always respond to "Tudo bem?" with "Tudo bem, obrigado/a" before asking back.

situations.title: Ordering in a Café
situations.summary: Learn to order coffee, bread, and pastries in a local snack bar.
goals: Learn the names of local coffee variations

cultural_notes.title: Ordering in a Café
cultural_notes.body: Ordering coffee in Madeira is specific. A "bica" is your standard espresso. If you want a bit of milk, ask for a "garoto".

situations.title: Numbers 1-10 + Connectors
situations.summary: Master basic numbers and start connecting your thoughts with "e", "mas", "porque".
goals: Use basic conjunctions to form longer sentences

cultural_notes.title: Numbers 1-10 + Connectors
cultural_notes.body: Numbers are the backbone of daily transactions. Connectors like "porque" help you explain your choices, making you sound more natural.

situations.title: Confusion & Clarification
situations.summary: Build your escape hatch. Learn how to handle fast speech and ask for repetition.

situations.title: Locations & Directions
situations.summary: Navigate Madeira. Ask where things are and understand "em cima" and "em baixo".

situations.title: Opinions & Preferences
situations.summary: Express what you think. Use "acho que", "parece-me" and "gosto imenso".

situations.title: Week 1 Stress Test
situations.summary: Consolidate everything from Week 1. Performance under pressure.

situations.title: Self-Introduction
situations.summary: Introduce yourself: name, origin, occupation, and reason for being in Madeira.

situations.title: Time: Days & Hours
situations.summary: Learn days of the week, clock times, and the critical "há" vs "daqui a".

situations.title: Numbers 11-100 + Prices
situations.summary: Handle any price in any shop. Master European Portuguese number forms.

situations.title: First Past Tense
situations.summary: Start speaking about the past. Use "Eu" forms of high-frequency verbs.

situations.title: Adjectives + Ser/Estar
situations.summary: Describe the world. Master the permanent vs temporary "to be" distinction.

situations.title: Daily Routines
situations.summary: Describe your day using reflexive verbs and sequence connectors.

situations.title: Week 2 Stress Test
situations.summary: Consolidate Weeks 1 and 2. Performance under pressure.

situations.title: Talking About Others
situations.summary: Master the third person (ele/ela/eles/elas) and irregular past forms.
goals: Describe other people and their activities
Use third-person singular and plural correctly

cultural_notes.title: Talking About Others
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, "ele" and "ela" are used freely, but often dropped when context is clear. "Foi" can mean both "he/she went" and "he/she was".

situations.title: TER: Have, Need, Feel
situations.summary: Master the verb "ter" for possession, obligation, and physical states.

cultural_notes.title: TER: Have, Need, Feel
cultural_notes.body: "Estou constipado/a" means you have a cold. It is a very common false friend for English speakers!

situations.title: PODER & Polite Requests
situations.summary: Use "poder" for permission, offers, and polite requests.
goals: Make polite requests and ask for permission

cultural_notes.title: PODER & Polite Requests
cultural_notes.body: "Pode" is the standard polite way to ask for things in Portugal. It is not rude; it is the engine of politeness.

situations.title: Shopping: Clothes & Colours
situations.summary: Shop for clothes, describe colours, and compare items.
goals: Ask for clothing items, sizes, and colours
Use the fitting room and decide on purchases

cultural_notes.title: Shopping: Clothes & Colours
cultural_notes.body: Colours must agree with the gender of the noun. "Uma camisola vermelha" but "Um casaco vermelho".

situations.title: Health & Pharmacy
situations.summary: Describe symptoms, understand dosages, and handle pharmacy visits.

cultural_notes.title: Health & Pharmacy
cultural_notes.body: "Dói-me a cabeça" literally means "the head hurts me". This is the most natural way to express pain in Portuguese.

situations.title: Stronger Connectors
situations.summary: Add depth to your speech with "portanto", "aliás", and "afinal".
goals: Use sophisticated connectors to link complex thoughts
Sound more like a native speaker with "portanto"

cultural_notes.title: Stronger Connectors
cultural_notes.body: "Portanto" is used constantly in Madeira, often as a filler or thinking pause, similar to "so" in English.

situations.title: Week 3 Stress Test
situations.summary: Consolidate everything from Week 3. Performance under pressure.
goals: Review all Week 3 patterns and vocabulary

cultural_notes.title: Week 3 Stress Test
cultural_notes.body: You can now hold a sophisticated 3-minute conversation on any everyday topic. Parabéns!

situations.title: The Imperfect Tense
situations.summary: Describe ongoing past states, habitual past actions, and how things used to be.

phrase_patterns.base: Estava a ler quando o telefone tocou.

cultural_notes.title: The Imperfect Tense
cultural_notes.body: The imperfect describes ongoing past states or habits. Contrast it with the preterite (specific events).

situations.title: The Future: Going To & Will
situations.summary: Speak about the future confidently using ir + infinitive and simple future forms.

situations.title: Wishes, Conditionals & Polite Requests
situations.summary: Express wishes, hypotheticals, and polished polite requests.

situations.title: Restaurant: Ordering a Full Meal
situations.summary: Handle a complete restaurant interaction from start to finish.

phrase_patterns.base: Gostava de uma mesa para dois.

situations.title: Describing Your Home
situations.summary: Describe where you live, your apartment, and the neighbourhood.

situations.title: Numbers 100+ & Years
situations.summary: Handle numbers above 100 fluently: hundreds, thousands, years, and large quantities.

situations.title: Week 4 Stress Test
situations.summary: Consolidate everything from Week 4. Performance under pressure.

situations.title: Tu vs Você: Informal/Formal
situations.summary: Navigate the social distinction between tu (informal) and você (formal).

situations.title: All Persons: Complete Present Tense
situations.summary: Complete the present tense with nós (we) and a gente.

situations.title: All Persons: Preterite + Imperfect
situations.summary: Complete the past paradigm with tu and nós forms.

situations.title: Telephone & Formal Messages
situations.summary: Handle telephone calls, voicemail, and formal written messages.

phrase_patterns.base: O meu número de utente é...

situations.title: Opinions: Agreeing & Disagreeing
situations.summary: Agree enthusiastically, disagree politely, and express nuanced views.

phrase_patterns.base: Acho que não é bem assim.

situations.title: Collaborative Future
situations.summary: Make plans, negotiate arrangements, and coordinate with others.

situations.title: Week 5 Stress Test
situations.summary: Consolidate everything from Week 5. Performance under pressure.

situations.title: The Present Perfect
situations.summary: Describe actions that happened in the recent past and connect to the present.

situations.title: Reflexive Verbs All Persons
situations.summary: Extend reflexive verbs to all persons in present and past tenses.

situations.title: Emotions, Empathy & Comfort
situations.summary: Express and respond to emotional states naturally.

situations.title: Madeiran Geography & Culture
situations.summary: Speak about Madeira with depth and specificity.

phrase_patterns.base: É uma ilha única no mundo.
A paisagem é de cortar a respiração.

situations.title: Complex Sentences
situations.summary: Use embedded clauses, relative clauses, and causal chains.

phrase_patterns.base: É um café que fica perto.

situations.title: Giving Explanations
situations.summary: Explain things, follow explanations, and check understanding.

situations.title: Week 6 Stress Test
situations.summary: Consolidate everything from Week 6. Performance under pressure.

situations.title: Vocês: Addressing Groups
situations.summary: Address groups of people correctly using vocês.

situations.title: Future Subjunctive
situations.summary: Use natural fixed phrases for future conditionals and time clauses.

situations.title: The Personal Infinitive
situations.summary: Use the personal infinitive for actions with different subjects.

situations.title: Abstract Topics: Work & Decisions
situations.summary: Discuss professional topics, decisions, and plans.

situations.title: Formal Writing & Funchal
situations.summary: Draft formal messages and discuss Funchal local geography.

situations.title: Local Beaches & Hiking
situations.summary: Speak about Madeiran outdoor life with genuine local knowledge.

phrase_patterns.base: Para praia de areia, recomendo Calheta.
Em maio as flores estão em pleno.

situations.title: Week 7 Stress Test
situations.summary: Consolidate everything from Week 7. Performance under pressure.

situations.title: Speed & Automaticity
situations.summary: Everything from Weeks 1-2 at maximum speed. Eliminate hesitation.

situations.title: Tense System Mastery
situations.summary: Switch between all six tenses across all persons fluidly.

situations.title: Register & Social Fluency
situations.summary: Navigate tu, você, and vocês organically based on social cues.

situations.title: Professional & Formal
situations.summary: Master formal written communication and abstract reasoning.

situations.title: Madeira in Depth
situations.summary: Speak about Madeira with genuine local knowledge and emotional resonance.

phrase_patterns.base: O Pico é de cortar a respiração.

situations.title: Complex Structures
situations.summary: Eliminate hesitation from relative clauses, temporal chains, and subjunctives.

phrase_patterns.base: É uma ilha que me apaixonou.

situations.title: The Grand Stress Test
situations.summary: Months 1 & 2 Combined. 10-minute unscripted conversation on any topic.

tracks.name: Structured Course
tracks.goal: Follow the month-by-month structured Madeira Portuguese course
